title: Why Hurricane Helene’s Flash Floods Were So Deadly in Asheville | NOVA | PBS
creator: NOVA PBS Official
description: In September 2024, a combination of weather conditions triggered catastrophic flash floods around Asheville, North Carolina. But how did it happen–and why? Discover how a low-pressure system pulled Tropical Storm Helene inland to the Blue Ridge Mountains, which turned a moderate flood into an unprecedented disaster.
subject: North and Central America; United States; North Carolina; Floods

title: Tropical Storm Chantal causes deadly flooding in North Carolina
creator: CBS Mornings
description: Deadly flooding struck North Carolina after Tropical Storm Chantal dumped torrential rains. At least two people died when their cars were swept away by rushing floodwaters. CBS News meteorologist Rob Marciano has the latest.
subject: North and Central America; United States; North Carolina; Floods

title: Coastal flooding in Eastern North Carolina
creator: WCTI News 12
description: Heavy rainfall and gusty winds led to an overwash on Highway 12. The North Carolina Department of Transportation closed parts of the highway on Saturday. Some parts of Highway 12 near Cedar Island had water washing over the highway. In New Bern, some roads were impassable.
subject: North and Central America; United States; North Carolina; Floods
